title: Bridge Essay: A working model of neural activity and phenomenal experience in psychosis.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through A, working, model, of, neural.

**Aworking model of neural activity and phenomenal experience in psychosis.** — Mol Psychiatry. PMID: 38844531. DOI: 10.1038/s41380-024-02607-4. According to classical phenomenology, phenomenal experience is composed of perceptions (related to environmental stimuli) and imagery/ideas (unrelated to environmental stimuli). Intensity/vividness is supposed to represent the key phenomenal difference between perceptions and ideas, higher in perceptions than ideas, and thus the core subjective criterion to distinguish reality from imagination.
